title: Enemy Pie
author: Derek Munson
description: A clever story about a boy who learns the fastest way to get rid of an enemy is to make him a friend.

sentences.englishOriginal: Welcome to BookPAL Storyline Online, brought to you by The Screen Actors Guild Foundation.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 歡迎來到由美國影視演員協會基金會為您呈現的 BookPAL Storyline Online。

phraseNotes.title: brought to you by
phraseNotes.explanation: 表示「由……贊助/提供/呈現」，常用於節目或活動的開場介紹。

examples.english: Today's show is brought to you by our local sponsors.
examples.chinese: 今天的節目是由我們當地的贊助商為您呈現。

sentences.englishOriginal: My name is Camryn Manheim.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是卡姆琳·曼海姆。

sentences.englishOriginal: You may know me from The Practice, but I hope not, because it was on way past your bedtime.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你可能從《律政風雲》這部影集認識我，但我希望沒有，因為那播放的時間早就超過你們的睡覺時間了。

phraseNotes.title: past one's bedtime
phraseNotes.explanation: 超過某人的上床睡覺時間。

examples.english: It is way past your bedtime, young man.
examples.chinese: 年輕人，早就超過你的睡覺時間了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: way past
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 「way」在這裡充當副詞，用來加強語氣，表示「遠遠超越」或「早就過了」。

examples.english: It's way past midnight, we should go home.
examples.chinese: 早就過午夜了，我們該回家了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Today, I'm going to read Enemy Pie, written by Derek Munson and illustrated by Tara Callahan King.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 今天，我要朗讀由德里克·芒森撰寫、塔拉·卡拉漢·金繪圖的《敵人派》。

vocabularyNotes.title: illustrate
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意為「為（書籍等）畫插圖」。繪本常見其過去分詞「illustrated by」。

examples.english: This children's book was beautifully illustrated by a local artist.
examples.chinese: 這本兒童繪本由一位在地藝術家精心繪製插圖。

sentences.englishOriginal: It should have been a perfect summer.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這本該是一個完美的夏天。

grammarNotes.title: should have + past participle
grammarNotes.explanation: 「should have + 過去分詞」表示「過去本來應該做或發生某事，但實際上卻沒有發生」，帶有遺憾或與事實相反的語氣。

examples.english: I should have studied harder for the exam.
examples.chinese: 我當初考試本該更努力準備的。

sentences.englishOriginal: My dad helped me build a treehouse in the backyard.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我爸爸幫我在後院蓋了一座樹屋。

sentences.englishOriginal: My sister was at camp for three whole weeks and I was on the best baseball team in town.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我姊姊去參加夏令營整整三個星期，而且我還加入了鎮上最好的棒球隊。

sentences.englishOriginal: It should have been the perfect summer, but it wasn't.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這本該是個完美的夏天，但事實並非如此。

sentences.englishOriginal: It was all good until Jeremy Ross moved into the neighborhood, right next door to my best friend Stanley.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 一切都很美好，直到傑瑞米·羅斯搬進這個社區，就住在了我好朋友史丹利的隔壁。

phraseNotes.title: right next door
phraseNotes.explanation: 表示「就在隔壁」，「right」有強調精確位置的語感。

examples.english: She lives right next door to the bakery.
examples.chinese: 她就住在麵包店的正隔壁。

sentences.englishOriginal: I did not like Jeremy Ross.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我一點也不喜歡傑瑞米·羅斯。

sentences.englishOriginal: He laughed at me when he struck me out at the baseball game.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當他在棒球比賽中把我三振出局時，他竟然嘲笑我。

phraseNotes.title: strike someone out
phraseNotes.explanation: 棒球術語，意為「將某人三振出局」。

examples.english: The pitcher struck out three batters in a row.
examples.chinese: 投手連續三振了三名打者。

sentences.englishOriginal: He had a party on his trampoline, I wasn't even invited, but my best friend Stanley was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他在彈簧床上舉辦派對，我連邀請都沒收到，但我最好的朋友史丹利卻受邀了。

vocabularyNotes.title: trampoline
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「彈簧床、跳床」。

examples.english: The kids are jumping on the trampoline in the garden.
examples.chinese: 孩子們正在花園的彈簧床上跳躍。

sentences.englishOriginal: Jeremy Ross was the one and only person on my enemy list.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 傑瑞米·羅斯是我敵人名單上唯二不選的唯一一人。

phraseNotes.title: one and only
phraseNotes.explanation: 固定片語，意為「獨一無二的、唯一的」。

examples.english: Please welcome the one and only Stevie Wonder!
examples.chinese: 請歡迎獨一無二的史提夫·汪達！

sentences.englishOriginal: I never even had an enemy list until he moved into the neighborhood, but as soon as he came along, I needed one.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在他搬到這個社區之前，我甚至從來沒有過敵人名單，但他一出現，我就需要一張了。

phraseNotes.title: as soon as
phraseNotes.explanation: 連詞片語，意為「一……就……」。

examples.english: I will call you as soon as I arrive.
examples.chinese: 我一到達就會給你打電話。

sentences.englishOriginal: I hung it up in my treehouse, where Jeremy Ross was not allowed to go.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我把它掛在我的樹屋裡，那是一個不允許傑瑞米·羅斯進入的地方。

grammarNotes.title: non-defining relative clause with where
grammarNotes.explanation: 「, where...」為非限定關聯副詞子句，用來補充說明前面提及的地點（樹屋）。

examples.english: We visited the park, where we had a picnic.
examples.chinese: 我們去逛了公園，並在那裡野餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: Dad understood stuff like enemies.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爸爸很懂敵人這類的事情。

sentences.englishOriginal: He told me that when he was my age, he had enemies too, but he knew a good way to get rid of them.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他告訴我，當他像我這麼大時，也有過敵人，但他知道一個擺脫他們的好方法。

phraseNotes.title: get rid of
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「擺脫、除去、擺平」。

examples.english: We need to get rid of these old boxes.
examples.chinese: 我們需要清理掉這些舊箱子。

sentences.englishOriginal: And I asked him to tell me how.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我便請他告訴我該怎麼做。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Tell you how? I'll show you how," he said, and he pulled a really old recipe book off the kitchen shelf.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「告訴你怎麼做？我做給你看，」他說著，從廚房架子上抽出一本非常古老的食譜。

vocabularyNotes.title: recipe
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「食譜、烹飪配方」。

examples.english: She gave me a great recipe for chocolate cake.
examples.chinese: 她給了我一個很讚的巧克力蛋糕食譜。

sentences.englishOriginal: And inside, there was a worn-out scrap of paper with faded writing.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 裡面夾著一張字跡模糊、破破爛爛的小紙條。

vocabularyNotes.title: worn-out
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「破舊的、磨損的」。

examples.english: He threw away his worn-out shoes.
examples.chinese: 他扔掉了他那雙穿破的鞋子。

sentences.englishOriginal: And Dad held it up when he squinted at it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爸爸把它舉起來，瞇起眼睛仔細端詳。

vocabularyNotes.title: squint
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「瞇著眼睛看」。

examples.english: He squinted at the screen to read the fine print.
examples.chinese: 他瞇著眼睛看螢幕以閱讀細小的字體。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Enemy Pie," he said, satisfied.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「敵人派，」他滿意地說道。

sentences.englishOriginal: You may be wondering what exactly is in Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你可能會好奇，敵人派裡面究竟加了些什麼。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was wondering too, but Dad said the recipe was so secret he couldn't even tell me.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我也很好奇，但爸爸說這個食譜是絕密，連我都不能告訴。

sentences.englishOriginal: I decided it must be magic, and I begged him to tell me something, anything.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我認定這一定是某種魔法，便哀求他透漏一點點資訊，什麼都好。

vocabularyNotes.title: beg
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「乞求、懇求」。常見句型為 beg someone to do something。

examples.english: The child begged his parents for a new toy.
examples.chinese: 那孩子懇求父母買一個新玩具。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I'll tell you this," he said, "Enemy Pie is the fastest known way to get rid of enemies."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我可以告訴你這點，」他說，「敵人派是已知能最快擺脫敵人的方法。」

nativeExpressionNotes.title: I'll tell you this
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中用來引起對方注意，準備發表重要論點或強調某事的開場白。

examples.english: I'll tell you this: hard work always pays off.
examples.chinese: 我跟你說：努力終究會有回報的。

sentences.englishOriginal: Of course, that got my mind working.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當然，這讓我的腦袋開始飛速運轉。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: got my mind working
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 表示「觸發了某人的思考、讓人腦筋轉了起來」。

examples.english: His question really got my mind working.
examples.chinese: 他的問題真的讓我的腦袋開始思考了起來。

sentences.englishOriginal: What kind of things, disgusting things, would I put into a pie for an enemy?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我會想在做給敌人的派裡面放些什麼樣的東西、多噁心的東西呢？

vocabularyNotes.title: disgusting
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「令人作嘔的、極其噁心的」。

examples.english: There was a disgusting smell coming from the trash can.
examples.chinese: 垃圾桶裡散發出一股令人作嘔的氣味。

sentences.englishOriginal: I brought Dad some weeds from the garden; he just shook his head.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我從花園拔了一些雜草給爸爸；他只是搖了搖頭。

sentences.englishOriginal: I brought him earthworms and rocks, but he didn't think he needed those.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我拿了蚯蚓和石頭給他，但他覺得用不上這些。

vocabularyNotes.title: earthworm
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「蚯蚓」。

examples.english: Birds often look for earthworms after it rains.
examples.chinese: 鳥類常在下雨後尋找蚯蚓。

sentences.englishOriginal: I gave him the gum that I'd been chewing on all morning; he gave it right back to me.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我把我咬了一整早的口香糖拿給他；他立刻原封不動地退還給我。

grammarNotes.title: past perfect continuous
grammarNotes.explanation: 「had been chewing」為過去完成進行式，強調在過去某個時間點之前，一直在持續進行的動作。

examples.english: It had been raining for hours before the sun finally came out.
examples.chinese: 在太陽終於出來之前，雨已經持續下了好幾個小時。

sentences.englishOriginal: I went out to play alone.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我獨自出去玩。

sentences.englishOriginal: I shot baskets until the ball got stuck up on the roof.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我投籃投到球卡在屋頂上為止。

phraseNotes.title: get stuck
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「被卡住、陷住，無法移動」。

examples.english: The car got stuck in the mud.
examples.chinese: 汽車卡在泥濘中了。

sentences.englishOriginal: I threw a boomerang and it never came back to me.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我扔了迴旋鏢，結果它再也沒有飛回來。

vocabularyNotes.title: boomerang
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「迴旋鏢、飛去來器」。

examples.english: Learning how to throw a boomerang takes a lot of practice.
examples.chinese: 學會如何擲迴旋鏢需要大量的練習。

sentences.englishOriginal: And all the while, I listened to my dad chopping and stirring and blending the ingredients of Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在此期間，我一直聽著爸爸在那裡切碎、攪拌、混合敵人派的食材。

vocabularyNotes.title: ingredient
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「（烹飪的）食材、成分」。

examples.english: Mix all the dry ingredients together in a bowl.
examples.chinese: 將所有乾性食材在碗中混合均勻。

phraseNotes.title: all the while
phraseNotes.explanation: 片語，意為「在此期間、一直」。

examples.english: She was talking on the phone, and all the while I was waiting for her.
examples.chinese: 她在打電話，而我自始至終都在等她。

sentences.englishOriginal: This could be a great summer after all.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 看來這個夏天終究還是會很棒的。

phraseNotes.title: after all
phraseNotes.explanation: 副詞片語，意為「終究、畢竟」，常用於事情發展與最初想像不同時的轉折語句。

examples.english: The weather was bad in the morning, but we had a nice picnic after all.
examples.chinese: 早晨天氣不好，但我們最終還是進行了一場很棒的野餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: Enemy Pie was going to be awful.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 敵人派肯定會非常糟糕。

sentences.englishOriginal: I tried to imagine how horrible it must smell, or worse yet, what it was going to look like.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我試著想像它聞起來會有多可怕，或者更糟的是，它看起來會長什麼樣子。

phraseNotes.title: worse yet
phraseNotes.explanation: 常用於補充說明「更糟糕的是……」。

examples.english: It started to rain, and worse yet, I forgot my umbrella.
examples.chinese: 下起雨了，更糟糕的是，我還忘了帶傘。

sentences.englishOriginal: But when I was in the backyard looking for ladybugs, I smelled something really, really, really good.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但是當我在後院找瓢蟲時，我聞到了一股非常、非常、非常香的味道。

vocabularyNotes.title: ladybug
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「瓢蟲」。

examples.english: The little girl found a ladybug on a leaf.
examples.chinese: 小女孩在葉子上發現了一隻瓢蟲。

sentences.englishOriginal: And as far as I could tell, it was coming from our kitchen.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 據我判斷，那香味是從我們家廚房飄出來的。

phraseNotes.title: as far as I could tell
phraseNotes.explanation: 表達「據我所知、就我所能看出來/判斷的而言」。

examples.english: As far as I could tell, everyone had a great time at the party.
examples.chinese: 就我所見，大家在派對上都玩得很開心。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was a bit confused.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我感到有些困惑。

sentences.englishOriginal: I went in to ask my dad what was wrong.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我走進屋裡問爸爸哪裡出了問題。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Enemy Pie shouldn't smell this good."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「敵人派不應該闻起來這麼香啊。」

sentences.englishOriginal: But Dad was smart.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但爸爸很聰明。

sentences.englishOriginal: "If Enemy Pie smelled bad, your enemy would never eat it," he said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「如果敵人派聞起來很難聞，你的敵人就絕對不會吃了，」他說。

grammarNotes.title: second conditional (hypothetical)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「If + 過去式動詞, ... would + 原形動詞」與現在事實相反的假設語氣。

examples.english: If I had more time, I would learn how to play the piano.
examples.chinese: 如果我有更多時間，我就會去學彈鋼琴。

sentences.englishOriginal: I could tell he had made Enemy Pie before.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我看得出來他以前一定做過敵人派。

phraseNotes.title: could tell
phraseNotes.explanation: 意為「看得出來、能判斷出」。

examples.english: I could tell she was nervous by the way she spoke.
examples.chinese: 從她說話的方式我看得出來她很緊張。

sentences.englishOriginal: The buzzer rang, and Dad put on the oven mitts and pulled the pie out of the oven.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 計時器響了，爸爸戴上隔熱手套，把派從烤箱裡拉了出來。

vocabularyNotes.title: oven mitts
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞（複數），指「烤箱隔熱手套」。

examples.english: Always use oven mitts when taking hot dishes out of the oven.
examples.chinese: 從烤箱拿出熱端盤時，一定要戴上烤箱手套。

sentences.englishOriginal: It looked like plain old pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它看起來就像個普普通通的普通派。

phraseNotes.title: plain old
phraseNotes.explanation: 用於口語，強調「普普通通的、沒什麼特別的」。

examples.english: It wasn't a fancy car, just a plain old sedan.
examples.chinese: 那不是什麼豪華車，就只是一輛普普通通的房車。

sentences.englishOriginal: It looked good enough to eat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它看起來相當美味可口。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was catching on, but still, I wasn't really sure how this Enemy Pie worked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我開始漸漸有點明白了，但我還是不太確定這個敵人派究竟是怎麼發揮作用的。

phraseNotes.title: catch on
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語片語，意為「理解、領會、開始明白」。

examples.english: He was slow at first, but he is starting to catch on.
examples.chinese: 他一開始有點慢，但現在開始漸漸懂了。

sentences.englishOriginal: What exactly did it do to enemies?
sentences.chineseTranslation: 它到底會對敵人造成什麼影響？

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe it made their hair fall out, or their breath stinky.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 也許它會讓他們的頭髮掉光，或者讓他們的口氣變臭。

phraseNotes.title: fall out
phraseNotes.explanation: 指（頭髮、牙齒等）「脫落、掉落」。

examples.english: His hair started to fall out as he got older.
examples.chinese: 隨著年齡增長，他的頭髮開始脫落。

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe it made bullies cry.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 也許它能把霸凌者給哭。

vocabularyNotes.title: bully
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「霸凌者、欺負弱小的人」。

examples.english: The school has a strict policy against bullies.
examples.chinese: 這所學校對霸凌者實行嚴格的反對政策。

sentences.englishOriginal: I asked my dad, but he was no help.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我問了爸爸，但他完全不幫忙解惑。

phraseNotes.title: be no help
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「毫無幫助、派不上用場」。

examples.english: I tried asking him for directions, but he was no help.
examples.chinese: 我試著向他問路，但他完全幫不上忙。

sentences.englishOriginal: He wouldn't tell me a thing.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他一個字也不肯透漏。

sentences.englishOriginal: But while the pie cooled, he filled me in on my job.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 不過在派冷卻的時候，他向我詳細說明了我該負責的工作。

phraseNotes.title: fill someone in
phraseNotes.explanation: 意為「向某人提供資訊、向某人交代詳情」。

examples.english: Can you fill me in on what happened at the meeting?
examples.chinese: 你能向我透漏一下會議上發生了什麼事嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: He talked quietly.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他壓低聲音說。

sentences.englishOriginal: "There's one part of Enemy Pie that I can't do.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「敵人派有一個環節是我無法替你做的。

sentences.englishOriginal: And in order for it to work, you need to spend a day with your enemy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 為了讓它奏效，你必須花一整天的時間陪你的敵人。

phraseNotes.title: in order for ... to
phraseNotes.explanation: 表示目的，意為「為了讓（某人/某事）能夠……」。

examples.english: In order for the machine to work, you must press this green button.
examples.chinese: 為了讓機器運作，你必須按下這個綠色按鈕。

sentences.englishOriginal: Even worse, you have to be nice to him.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 更慘的是，你還必須對他非常好。

sentences.englishOriginal: It's not easy, but that's the only way Enemy Pie can work.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這並不容易，但這是敵人派能發揮作用的唯一方法。

sentences.englishOriginal: Are you sure you want to go through with this?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你確定你要貫徹到底執行這件事嗎？」

phraseNotes.title: go through with
phraseNotes.explanation: 表示「把（艱難或不愉快的計畫）堅持做到底、貫徹執行」。

examples.english: He decided to go through with the surgery despite his fears.
examples.chinese: 儘管害怕，他還是決定照原計畫進行手術。

sentences.englishOriginal: Of course I was.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我當然確定。

sentences.englishOriginal: It sounded horrible, it was scary, but it was worth a try.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這聽起來很可怕，也很嚇人，但值得一試。

phraseNotes.title: worth a try
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「值得一試」。

examples.english: I don't know if this method will work, but it's worth a try.
examples.chinese: 我不知道這個方法行不行得通，但值得一試。

sentences.englishOriginal: All I had to do was spend one day with Jeremy Ross, and then he'd be out of my hair for the rest of my life.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我唯一需要做的就是陪傑瑞米·羅斯待上一天，然後在我的餘生中他就再也不會來煩我了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: out of my hair
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 非常道地的口語成語，表示「不再打擾我、不再給我添麻煩/惹事」。

examples.english: I gave the kids some coloring books to keep them out of my hair.
examples.chinese: 我給了孩子們一些塗色本，好讓他們別來打擾我。

sentences.englishOriginal: I rode my bike to his house and I knocked on the door.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我騎著自行車去他家，敲了敲門。

sentences.englishOriginal: When Jeremy opened the door, he seemed surprised.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當傑瑞米開門時，他看起來很驚訝。

sentences.englishOriginal: He stood at the other side of the screen door and he looked at me, waiting for me to say something.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他站在紗門的另一邊看著我，等著我開口。

vocabularyNotes.title: screen door
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指美式住宅常見的「紗門」。

examples.english: Please close the screen door so mosquitoes won't get in.
examples.chinese: 請把紗門關上，這樣蚊子才不會進來。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was nervous.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我當時很緊張。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Uh, can you play?" I asked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「額，你能出來玩嗎？」我問道。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he looked confused.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他看起來一臉困惑。

sentences.englishOriginal: "I'll go ask my mom," he said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我去問問我媽媽，」他說。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he came back with his shoes in his hand.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 接著他手上提著鞋子走了出來。

sentences.englishOriginal: His mom walked around the corner to say hello.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他媽媽從轉角走出來向我打招呼。

sentences.englishOriginal: "You boys stay out of trouble," she said, smiling.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「你們這兩個小男孩可別惹麻煩喔，」她微笑著說。

phraseNotes.title: stay out of trouble
phraseNotes.explanation: 長輩對晚輩常說的告誡語，意為「乖乖的、別惹事、別闖禍」。

examples.english: Have fun at the park and stay out of trouble!
examples.chinese: 在公園玩得開心點，別闖禍喔！

sentences.englishOriginal: We rode bikes for a while and played on the trampoline.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們騎了一會兒自行車，又在彈簧床上玩耍。

sentences.englishOriginal: Then we made some water balloons and threw them at the neighborhood girls, but we missed.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 然後我們做了一些水氣球擲向社區裡的女孩子們，但我們都沒砸中。

sentences.englishOriginal: Jeremy's mom made us lunch, and after lunch, we went over to my house.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 傑瑞米的媽媽幫我們做好了午餐，吃完午餐後，我們就去了我家。

sentences.englishOriginal: It was strange.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這感覺很奇妙。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was kind of having fun with my enemy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我和我的敵人玩得居然還挺開心的。

sentences.englishOriginal: He almost seemed nice.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他看起來甚至有點友善。

sentences.englishOriginal: But of course, I couldn't tell my dad that, since he'd been working so hard to make the Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但當然，我不能把這件事告訴我爸爸，畢竟他花了大把心力在製作敵人派。

sentences.englishOriginal: Jeremy Ross liked my basketball hoop.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 傑瑞米·羅斯很喜歡我的籃框。

vocabularyNotes.title: basketball hoop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「籃框、籃框組」。

examples.english: They installed a basketball hoop in their driveway.
examples.chinese: 他們在自家車道上記裝了一個籃框。

sentences.englishOriginal: He said he wished that he had a basketball hoop, but they didn't have room for one.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他說他希望自己家也有個籃框，但他們家沒有空間擺設。

phraseNotes.title: have room for
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「有（空間/地方）放得下某物」。

examples.english: Do you have room for another passenger in your car?
examples.chinese: 你的車裡還有空間再坐一名乘客嗎？

sentences.englishOriginal: I let him win a game just to be nice.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 為了展現善意，我特地讓了他一局。

sentences.englishOriginal: Jeremy Ross knew how to throw a boomerang.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 傑瑞米·羅斯非常懂得怎麼甩迴旋鏢。

sentences.englishOriginal: He threw it and it came right back to him.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他丟出去後，迴旋鏢就精準地飛回了他身邊。

sentences.englishOriginal: I threw it and it went over my house into the backyard.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我扔出去，結果它飛過了我的房子，掉到了後院去。

sentences.englishOriginal: And when we climbed over the fence to find it, the first thing Jeremy noticed was my treehouse.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而當我們翻過籬笆去找鏢時，傑瑞米第一眼注意到的就是我的樹屋。

sentences.englishOriginal: My treehouse was my treehouse.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我的樹屋就是我獨立的私人領地。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: A is A
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 這種重複主詞的句型（如 My treehouse was my treehouse），是用來強調「這個東西完全屬於我，主權不容侵犯/規則由我定」。

examples.english: My room is my room, so please knock before entering.
examples.chinese: 我的房間就是我的私人空間，所以進來前請敲門。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was the boss.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 在那裡我是老大。

sentences.englishOriginal: If my sister wanted in, I didn't have to let her.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果我姊姊想進來，我不需要讓她進來。

phraseNotes.title: want in
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語簡略表達，意為「想要進來」或「想要加入」。

examples.english: The dog is standing by the door because he wants in.
examples.chinese: 狗狗站在門邊，因為牠想進來。

sentences.englishOriginal: If my dad wanted in, I didn't have to let him.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 如果我爸爸想進來，我也可以不讓他進來。

sentences.englishOriginal: And Jeremy Ross wanted in.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 而現在傑瑞米·羅斯想進去。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Can we go in it?" he asked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我們能進去裡面嗎？」他問。

sentences.englishOriginal: I knew he was going to ask me that.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我早就知道他會問我這個問題。

sentences.englishOriginal: But he was the top person, the only person on my enemy list, and enemies aren't allowed in my treehouse.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但他是我敵人名單上的第一名，也是唯一的一個人，而敵人是不被允許進入我的樹屋的。

sentences.englishOriginal: But he did teach me how to throw a boomerang, and he did have me over for lunch, and he did let me play on his trampoline.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但他確實教了我怎麼扔迴旋鏢，也確實請我吃了午餐，還讓我玩了他的彈簧床。

phraseNotes.title: have someone over
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「邀請某人到家中做客/作客」。

examples.english: We should have the neighbors over for dinner sometime.
examples.chinese: 我們找個時間應該請鄰居來家裡吃晚餐。

grammarNotes.title: emphatic 'did'
grammarNotes.explanation: 在肯定句中使用助動詞 did + 原形動詞，用來加強語氣，強調「確實、真的有做某事」。

examples.english: I did finish my homework before going out.
examples.chinese: 我在出去前真的有把作業做完。

sentences.englishOriginal: He wasn't being a very good enemy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他表現得實在不太像個合格的敵人。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Okay," I said, "but hold on."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「好吧，」我說，「但先等等。」

phraseNotes.title: hold on
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語常用片語，表示「等一下、稍等」。

examples.english: Hold on a second, I forgot my keys.
examples.chinese: 稍等一下，我忘了帶鑰匙。

sentences.englishOriginal: I climbed up ahead of him and I tore the enemy list off the wall.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我比他先爬了上去，把牆上的敵人名單給撕了下來。

vocabularyNotes.title: tear
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞（過去式為 tore），指「撕下、撕裂」。

examples.english: He tore the page out of the notebook.
examples.chinese: 他把那頁紙從筆記本上撕了下來。

sentences.englishOriginal: I had a checkerboard and some cards in the treehouse, and we played games until my dad called us down for dinner.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我的樹屋裡有一副西洋跳棋和一些撲克牌，我們一直玩遊戲，直到我爸爸叫我們下樓吃晚餐。

vocabularyNotes.title: checkerboard
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「西洋跳棋棋盤」。

examples.english: We set up the checkerboard on the coffee table.
examples.chinese: 我們在茶幾上擺好了西洋跳棋盤。

sentences.englishOriginal: We pretended we didn't hear him, but when he came out to get us, we tried to hide from him, but somehow he found us.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我們假裝沒聽到，但當他出來叫我們時，我們試圖躲起來，不知怎的還是被他找到了。

sentences.englishOriginal: Dad made us macaroni and cheese for dinner, my favorite.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爸爸晚餐做了起司通心麵給我們吃，那可是我的最愛。

vocabularyNotes.title: macaroni
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「通心粉、通心麵」。

examples.english: Macaroni and cheese is a classic comfort food.
examples.chinese: 起司通心麵是一道經典的美式療癒美食。

sentences.englishOriginal: It was Jeremy's favorite too.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 巧的是，那也是傑瑞米的最愛。

sentences.englishOriginal: Maybe Jeremy Ross wasn't so bad after all.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 也許傑瑞米·羅斯終究也沒那麼壞嘛。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was beginning to think that maybe we should just forget about the Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我開始覺得，也許我們乾脆直接忘掉敵人派這回事算了。

sentences.englishOriginal: But sure enough, after dinner, Dad brought out the pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但果然，晚餐過後，爸爸端出了那個派。

phraseNotes.title: sure enough
phraseNotes.explanation: 副詞片語，意為「不出所料、果然」。

examples.english: I thought it would rain, and sure enough, it started pouring.
examples.chinese: 我以為會下雨，果不其然，開始下起了傾盆大雨。

sentences.englishOriginal: And I watched as he cut it into eight thick slices.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我眼睜睜地看著他把派切成了八大塊切片。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Dad," I said, "it sure is nice to have a new friend in the neighborhood."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「爸爸，」我說，「社區裡能有一個新朋友真的挺不錯的。」

sentences.englishOriginal: I was trying to get his attention and trying to tell him that Jeremy Ross was no longer my enemy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我試圖引起他的注意，並試著告訴他傑瑞米·羅斯已經不再是我的敵人了。

phraseNotes.title: no longer
phraseNotes.explanation: 意為「不再……」。

examples.english: He no longer lives in this town.
examples.chinese: 他不再住在這個鎮上了。

sentences.englishOriginal: But Dad only smiled and nodded.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但爸爸只是笑了笑，點了點頭。

sentences.englishOriginal: I think he thought I was just pretending.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我想他以為我只是在配合裝裝樣子而已。

sentences.englishOriginal: Dad dished up three plates side by side with big pieces of pie and giant scoops of ice cream.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爸爸盛好了三盤派排在一起，上面放著大塊的派和巨型球冰淇淋。

vocabularyNotes.title: scoop
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「（冰淇淋等的）一勺/一球」。

examples.english: I'd like two scoops of chocolate ice cream, please.
examples.chinese: 請給我兩球巧克力冰淇淋。

phraseNotes.title: dish up
phraseNotes.explanation: 指「盛盤、分發（食物）」。

examples.english: Mom is dishing up the soup for dinner.
examples.chinese: 媽媽正在盛湯準備吃晚餐。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he passed one to me and one to Jeremy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 接著他遞了一盤給我，一盤給傑瑞米。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Wow," Jeremy said, looking at the pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「哇，」傑瑞米看著派驚嘆道。

sentences.englishOriginal: "My dad never makes pie like this."
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「我爸爸從來沒做過這種派。」

sentences.englishOriginal: It was at this point that I panicked.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 就在這個當口，我陷入了恐慌。

vocabularyNotes.title: panic
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「恐慌、驚慌失措」。

examples.english: Don't panic! Everything will be fine.
examples.chinese: 別驚慌！一切都會沒事的。

sentences.englishOriginal: I didn't want Jeremy to eat Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我不希望傑瑞米吃下敵人派。

sentences.englishOriginal: He was my friend.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他是我的朋友啊。

sentences.englishOriginal: I couldn't let him eat it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我絕不能讓他吃下去。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Jeremy, don't eat it! It's bad pie! I think it's poisonous or something!"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「傑瑞米，別吃！那是個壞派！我覺得裡面有毒還是什麼的！」

vocabularyNotes.title: poisonous
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「有毒的」。

examples.english: Some wild mushrooms are extremely poisonous.
examples.chinese: 某些野生蘑菇含有劇毒。

sentences.englishOriginal: And Jeremy's fork stopped before reaching his mouth.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 傑瑞米的叉子在碰到嘴巴前硬生生地停住了。

sentences.englishOriginal: And he crumpled his eyebrows, and he looked at me funny.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他皺起眉頭，用怪異的眼神看著我。

phraseNotes.title: look at someone funny
phraseNotes.explanation: 口語表達，指「用怪異/疑惑的目光看著某人」。

examples.english: Why is everyone looking at me funny?
examples.chinese: 為什麼大家都要用怪異的眼神看著我？

sentences.englishOriginal: I felt so relieved.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我感到無比的鬆了一口氣。

vocabularyNotes.title: relieved
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，指「感到寬慰的、放心的」。

examples.english: She was relieved to hear that her family was safe.
examples.chinese: 得知家人平安無事後，她鬆了一口氣。

sentences.englishOriginal: I had saved his life.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我救了他的命。

sentences.englishOriginal: I was a hero.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我是個英雄。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Well, if it's so bad," Jeremy asked, "then why is your dad already eating half of it?"
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「嗯，如果真有那麼糟糕的話，」傑瑞米問道，「那為什麼你爸爸已經吃掉半個了？」

sentences.englishOriginal: Well, I turned to look at my dad.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 嗯，我轉頭看向我爸爸。

sentences.englishOriginal: Sure enough, he was eating Enemy Pie.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 果不其然，他正在大口大口地吃著敵人派。

sentences.englishOriginal: "Good stuff," he mumbled through a mouthful.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 「真是好東西，」他嘴裡塞滿派，含糊不清地嘟囔著。

vocabularyNotes.title: mumble
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「咕噥、含糊不清地說話」。

examples.english: He mumbled an apology and walked away.
examples.chinese: 他含糊地道了個歉便走開了。

phraseNotes.title: through a mouthful
phraseNotes.explanation: 形容嘴裡塞滿食物時邊吃邊說話的樣子。

examples.english: Don't try to talk through a mouthful of food.
examples.chinese: 嘴裡塞滿食物時不要試圖說話。

sentences.englishOriginal: And that was all he said.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 這就是他所說的全部分了。

sentences.englishOriginal: And I sat there watching him eating Enemy Pie for a few seconds.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我就坐在那裡看著他吃了幾秒鐘的敵人派。

sentences.englishOriginal: Dad was laughing, Jeremy was happily eating, and neither of them was losing any hair.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 爸爸笑得開心，傑瑞米也吃得很歡，而且他們兩個誰都沒有掉頭髮。

grammarNotes.title: neither of + plural noun
grammarNotes.explanation: 「neither of + 複數名詞」表示「兩者皆不」，文法上通常搭配單數動詞（如 was）。

examples.english: Neither of my brothers was at the party.
examples.chinese: 我的兩個兄弟當時都沒在派對上。

sentences.englishOriginal: It seemed safe enough, so I took a tiny taste.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 看起來似乎夠安全，所以我輕輕嘗了一小口。

sentences.englishOriginal: Enemy Pie was delicious.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 敵人派簡直美味極了。

sentences.englishOriginal: After dessert, Jeremy rode his bike home, but not before inviting me over to play on his trampoline in the morning.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 甜點過後，傑瑞米騎著自行車回家了，但在走之前他不忘邀請我明早去他家玩彈簧床。

phraseNotes.title: but not before
phraseNotes.explanation: 用於描述兩件事時，表示「但在做完某事之後才……/在做某事之前先完成了另一件事」。

examples.english: He left the office, but not before finishing all his report work.
examples.chinese: 他離開了辦公室，但在離開前先把所有的報告工作都處理完了。

sentences.englishOriginal: He said he was going to teach me how to flip.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他說他打算教我怎麼做空翻。

vocabularyNotes.title: flip
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞/動詞，在彈簧床的情境中指「空翻（動作）」。

examples.english: He did a back flip into the swimming pool.
examples.chinese: 他向後空翻跳進了游泳池。

sentences.englishOriginal: As for Enemy Pie, I still don't know how to make it.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 至於敵人派嘛，我到現在依然不知道該怎麼做。

phraseNotes.title: as for
phraseNotes.explanation: 介詞片語，意為「至於、關於」。

examples.english: As for the money, don't worry about it.
examples.chinese: 至於錢的事情，你就別擔心了。

sentences.englishOriginal: I still wonder if enemies really do hate it, or if their hair falls out or their breath turns bad.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我依然很好奇敵人是否真的會討厭它，或者他們的頭髮是否真的會掉光、口氣是否會變臭。

sentences.englishOriginal: I don't know if I'll ever get an answer, because I just lost my best enemy.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 我不知道我是否能找到答案，因為我剛剛才失去了我最好的敵人。
